summary.intent: Set up an automated PR monitoring workflow for PR #93 that watches for review comments, CI failures, and merge conflicts, then automatically applies fixes and responds.
summary.outcome: Successfully established a 60-second polling monitor for PR #93; detected and resolved a gemini-code-assist review suggesting alphabetical ordering of tools in build-entire/.tool-versions, committed and pushed the fix.

repo: Repo is ronnnnn/cc; PR #93 is titled 'build: entire 0.5.5 を .tool-versions に追加'
gemini-code-assist is active as an automated reviewer on this repo
build-entire/.tool-versions requires tools listed in alphabetical order per project convention

code.finding: Tool entries must be sorted alphabetically; gemini-code-assist enforces this via automated review comments

workflow: Monitor tool events use pipe-separated format (e.g., NEW_REVIEWS|PRRT_kwDOQFge-M57bfoI) where the second field is the node ID of the review thread
A shell script written to /tmp/pr-monitor-{number}.sh drives the polling logic
State variables (UNFIXABLE_RUNS, REVIEW_COMMITS, CI_COMMITS, etc.) are tracked to accumulate session-level metrics

friction: Required ToolSearch before Monitor could be used, indicating schema for Monitor is deferred and not pre-loaded
Prior tasks had to be bulk-updated/cleared before new workflow tasks could be created, suggesting task state was already dirty at session start
open_items: CI failure handling path was set up but not yet exercised — untested branch of the automation
Conflict resolution logic is wired into the monitor script but has not been triggered or validated
Re-requesting reviewers after fixes (RE_REQUESTED_REVIEWERS state variable) is tracked but no reviewer was re-requested in this session
Monitoring is ephemeral (tied to the session); no persistent cron or scheduled trigger was created for the workflow
